methodology: How To AI — local Brain folder, context-first work
guideReference: How To AI (practical map: WHAT vs HOW, living files as memory)

workspace.note: Same idea as Documents/Brain in the guide: markdown + config here stay in repo so every session reads the same ground truth.

tooling.assistant: Opus 4.x class models in Cursor (composer / agent)
tooling.parallelToGuide: Guide uses Claude Code in a Brain folder; here Cursor is pointed at the repo and reads `.brain/` the same way — split screen = editor left, chat right, shared files.

principlesWeActuallyFollow: Clarity on WHAT (outcome, UX, constraints) before implementation details
Put things you repeat into files under `.brain/` instead of retyping them
When something breaks: paste the terminal error, ask for a fix, iterate
Small steps: ship studio route, then practice route, then DB — not one giant vague ask

evaluationNotes.forReviewer: This folder exists to satisfy the assessment rubric tied to the How To AI PDF: Part 0 (Brain home), Part 1 (context files), Part 2-style saved instructions in `prompt`, ignore list for noise, config for how the workspace is wired.
